summary: Diane Hendricks discusses the biggest risk she took during the 2008 financial crisis: acquiring Bradco. She explains the painful decision to temporarily give up over 40% of her company's equity to investment partners to fund the deal, ultimately buying her shares back in three and a half years by trusting her team's expertise.

principles.statement: Trust your internal experts to execute difficult decisions.
principles.rationale: Because you cannot predict the future, especially during a crisis, you must rely on a great team to get things done.
principles.source_quote: you can't see into the future so it was it was it was difficult but I have a great team had a great team then and everybody believed that we could get it done and I you have to trust trust your experts within the companies

principles.statement: Be willing to temporarily give up control for a necessary acquisition, provided you have a plan to regain it.
principles.rationale: Sometimes a strategic acquisition is too big to handle alone, requiring outside capital. If you know the target company is good, you can partner with investment bankers, give them their return, and buy your shares back.
principles.source_quote: it really is painful to give up control and to bring in investment bankers but if I could give them the return like they always want to return within five years I would buy my shares back

heuristics: Trust your experts within the companies that you have.

signature_quotes.text: it really is painful to give up control and to bring in investment bankers but if I could give them the return like they always want to return within five years I would buy my shares back
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the financing of the Bradco acquisition during the 2008 recession.

signature_quotes.text: you can't see into the future so it was it was it was difficult but I have a great team had a great team then and everybody believed that we could get it done
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the uncertainty of making a massive acquisition during a financial crisis.

signature_quotes.text: it's not a company that's ever going to be for sale
signature_quotes.context: Explaining her long-term vision for her family-owned business.

anti_patterns.description: Permanently giving up control or selling the family business.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: She views her business as a legacy for her children to run, making permanent loss of control or selling the company an unacceptable outcome.
